title: indulgent crunchy middle-eastern fish flatbread
description: crispy fish flatbread with a crunchy crust, perfect for a satisfying solo dinner. inspired by middle-eastern flavors.

ingredients.name: fish fillet (such as cod or tilapia)

instructions: in a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and pepper.
gradually add yogurt and water to the flour mixture, stirring with a fork until a shaggy dough forms.
turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
divide the dough into 2 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
place a fish fillet in the center of each circle and top with lemon juice, garlic powder, and paprika.
sprinkle crushed sumac over the fish and fold the dough over to form a flatbread.
brush with olive oil and place under the broiler for 2-3 minutes, until golden brown and crispy.
remove from the oven and immediately top with crunchy sumac flatbread topping (see notes).
